compatibility.semantic_truth_boundary: QIF validator and profile compatibility checks can prove structure, traceability, reference resolution, confidence reproducibility, and rule compliance. They do not prove semantic truth, market truth, expert correctness, or operator comprehension.

not_allowed: Do not vendor QIF schemas or docs into AOF as the source of truth.
Do not claim QIF validator pass proves semantic quality truth.
Do not treat tests, artifact count, actor count, dashboard existence, or release tags as quality by themselves.
Do not upgrade QIF compatibility silently without recording the selected provider profile.

upgrade_policy.aof_upgrade_requirement: When QIF changes version, AOF must add or update a qif-provider-profile artifact, record compatibility boundaries, and run governance review before using the new profile for quality claims.
upgrade_policy.fallback: If the selected QIF version cannot be validated or mapped, AOF may continue using the last active provider profile but must mark new quality claims as profile-stale or governance-review-required.

governance.review_trigger: Trigger review when QIF version, source tag, adapter outputs, confidence policy, or semantic truth boundary changes.
governance.acceptance_gate: AOF quality claims must reference the active QIF provider profile and must explain which claims are structural, runtime-backed, operator-validated, externally validated, or not proven.
